name: bids
tagline: Reference/workflow guide for organizing, querying, validating, and converting neuroscience datasets (MRI/EEG/MEG/etc.) into BIDS format.
verdict: Clean, legitimate domain-reference skill (BIDS neuroimaging data standard), authored per metadata by a known BIDS community maintainer. No secret access, no network exfiltration, no shell-piped remote code, no hidden or manipulative instructions found — all commands are standard package installs (pip/uv, Deno, Docker) and documented CLI usage. Minor hygiene-

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do NOT use this for...' guidance is stated anywhere; scope is narrow enough (BIDS-specific) that this is a minor omission, not a real ambiguity risk.
At 756 lines the file is well over the ~500-line guidance, though content stays on-topic throughout rather than padding with irrelevant material.
File is 756 lines, exceeding the ~500-line size budget guidance.
